prose:
Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO (Koopmans) gap of formaldehyde, in electron-volts (eV).
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with default RI density-fitting in PySCF.
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user as XYZ file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz”; no re-optimization was performed (single-point `DFT//exp-XYZ`).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software/backend: PySCF (invoked via chemkit `frontier-orbitals`; precise version not provided by engine).
SCF convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings were reported by the engine; density fitting (RI) approximation was used by default.
Live log & reproducibility: The calculation streamed live output to the automatically named file “frontier_<timestamp>.out” in the working directory (announced at launch) and wrote its structured results to “/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgl1jd5b4/live.json”. The JSON’s `cli_invocation` field (shown below) contains the full command line used; rerunning it in the same environment reproduces the numbers.

Result
HOMO = −7.6523067 eV
LUMO = −1.7826355 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans definition) = 5.8696712 eV

No warnings were reported by the backend.

cli_invocation (for reproducibility):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgl1jd5b4/live.json

The result is a computed value obtained entirely from the above DFT calculation; no experimental comparison is offered.
